title: BV photometry of the ultracompact binary star GP Com
author_string: R. Zamanov, L. Dankova, M. Minev, D. Boneva, K. Yankova
abstract: We present optical B and V band photometry of GP Com - an ultracompact binary consisting of an accreting white dwarf and helium secondary component. Our data set contains 7.7 hours observations in V band with the 2.0m telescope and 2.9 hours simultaneous observations in B and V bands with the 1.5m telescope of the Rozhen National Astronomical Obsevatory, Bulgaria. The observations cover of about 13 orbital periods.   We find an orbital modulation with amplitude 0.04-0.05 mag in B and V bands. Adopting that it is due to a bright spot, we estimate its temperature 19700 \pm 3000 K. We estimate mass accretion rate onto the white dwarf of about 2.10^{-12} M_sun/yr, consistent with the predicted rate for a cool donor.   The data are available on Zenodo: zenodo.org/records/18768211.
comment: published in C. R. Acad. Bulg. Sci., 79, 285-293 (2026)
